summary.intent: Fix a CI failure on PR #704 in fogodev/ars-ui by correcting code formatting issues in RangeCalendar tests and getting the PR through automated checks including Codex review.
summary.outcome: Identified that the CI formatting failure was caused by nightly rustfmt's import grouping rule (blank line between `alloc` and `std`), applied `cargo +nightly fmt`, verified the fix locally, and pushed a corrected commit. The CI run was re-triggered and Codex review was re-requested.

repo: The fogodev/ars-ui repo uses nightly rustfmt for formatting checks, not stable — running `cargo fmt --check` locally with the stable toolchain will miss nightly-only rules and give false confidence.
Codex review is triggered by posting '@codex review' as a comment on the GitHub issue/PR.
The repo enforces import grouping with a blank line between `alloc` and `std` groups, a nightly-only rustfmt rule.

code.finding: Import grouping was missing a blank line between `alloc` and `std` imports, which is enforced by nightly rustfmt but not caught by stable rustfmt.

workflow: Always run `cargo +nightly fmt --check` (not stable) before pushing when a repo's CI uses nightly rustfmt — stable and nightly rustfmt can produce different results.
Use `gh pr checks <PR>` and `gh pr view <PR> --json ...` to monitor CI status programmatically.
Use `gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/issues/<number>/comments` to post bot trigger comments like Codex review requests.

friction: Local `cargo fmt --check` (stable) passed but CI failed because the repo requires nightly rustfmt — the stable toolchain silently masked the formatting issue.
Had to wait through a full CI cycle to discover the formatting failure, then push a second corrective commit.
open_items: Verify that the re-triggered CI run fully passes (coverage job and Codex review result were still pending at end of session).
Consider documenting or scripting the nightly rustfmt requirement so future contributors don't hit the same stable-vs-nightly mismatch.
